title: A Nineteenth-Century Countess’s Sultry Selfies
description: Virginia Oldoini helped conceptualize and starred in more than four hundred portraits so experimental and expressive that they have drawn comparisons to works by Claude Cahun and Cindy Sherman.
hook: A 19th-century countess made 400+ portraits so experimental they predicted Cindy Sherman by a century—selfies as radical self-invention.

title: Hollywood Isn’t Directly Attacking Trump. It’s Doing Something More Interesting.
description: Less “what we need right now” and more “what it feels like to be alive.”
hook: Instead of preaching, Hollywood's best films now just capture what it actually feels like to exist right now. That's more subversive.

title: More than Theoretical Girls: Adele Bertei on the Women of No Wave
description:
From working with Brian Eno to playing with The Contortions and The Bloods, Adele Bertei had a front-row seat to New York's infamous No Wave scene. She talks to Elizabeth Wiet about noise, melody, and why the fertile ecosystem of Downtown NYC couldn't come about today

Adele at CBGBs. Photo: Julia Gorton

As a critic, I’ve never had
hook: The women who made No Wave—from Brian Eno to The Contortions—finally get their story from someone who lived it.

title: Moral Mysteries
description:
“It is always a significant question to ask about any philosopher: what is he afraid of?” wrote Iris Murdoch in her slim work of moral philosophy, The Sovereignty of Good.

The post Moral Mysteries appeared first on The Point Magazine.
hook: What is a philosopher afraid of? Iris Murdoch's question unlocks why people believe what they do.

title: Sound Of Falling review – a complex puzzle box powered by the sensory and sensual
description: Mascha Schilinski’s beguiling drama follows four generations living in the same rural German farmhouse and the cyclical nature of their trauma.
hook: Four generations in one farmhouse; a sensory puzzle about how trauma repeats itself through texture, sound, and silence.

title: He Wrote Judy Blume’s Life Story. She Won’t Talk About It.
description: Mark Oppenheimer had many conversations with his subject for his new book. Then the relationship took a turn.
hook: A biographer spent years with Judy Blume. Then she shut him out completely. What went wrong?

title: ‘Esoteric Ebb’ Review: A Roguishly Charming, ‘Disco Elysium’-like Adventure
description:
To its immense credit, the choices you make across the game feel electrifying.

The post ‘Esoteric Ebb’ Review: A Roguishly Charming, ‘Disco Elysium’-like Adventure appeared first on
hook: A Disco Elysium-like game where every choice actually matters—rare enough that it deserves your attention.
